title: All about Battersea

quoteFirst: “After this awful scene, the firing was recommenced with the Ships to leeward of the Centre, till
quoteLast: , when there was a total cessation of firing for about ten minutes; after which it was revived till about three in the morning, when it again ceased. After the victory had been secured in the Van, such British ships as were in a condition to move, had gone down upon the fresh Ships of the Enemy, which occasioned these renewals of the fight, all of which terminated with the same happy success in favour of our Flag.
title: The Boys' Nelson

title: Avery

quoteLast: , a warm, sweet morning, with great hurrying back and forth at the ferry, women climbing to the open seats of the cable-cars, pinning on their violets or roses as they climbed. In the air was the pleasant mingling of the scents of roasting coffee, salt bay-water, and softening tar in the paving, that is native only to San Francisco.
title: Sisters
author: Norris, Kathleen Thompson, 1880-1966

quoteFirst: But the end comes to the man who lives to wait, and to-night, at
quoteLast: , LEWIS PELLY sitting bolt upright, awakened out of peaceful slumber by a sudden cheer; knew that the Land Bill was at last through Committee.

quoteFirst: He was most careful in pointing out that the evidence was entirely of a circumstantial character. On the one hand, there was the certainty that the crime was committed some time between half-past ten o'clock and half-past eleven, and the fact that the prisoner entered the Bank by means of his pass-key about
quoteLast: and did not reach home till close upon midnight; and that the account he gave of having walked all the way to Strong's house during that interval of time, and of having knocked at his door, was, so far as regarded the latter part of the prisoner's assertion, emphatically denied by Strong himself.
title: The Heart of a Mystery
author: Speight, T. W. (Thomas Wilkinson), 1830-1915

quoteLast: on the 22d, and after two days and two nights on the cars, reached Winnipeg, the capital of Manitoba. At the hotel there we found the rooms for which we had telegraphed ready for us.
title: Another Summer: The Yellowstone Park and Alaska
author: Gillis, Charles J.
